title: Sign in successfully with valid email and password and land on Dashboard
description: Verifies a user can sign in with correct credentials and is redirected to the authenticated dashboard.

title: Invalid password shows error and stays on Sign In page
description: Verifies an incorrect password shows the expected error and does not navigate away from the sign-in page.

steps.description: Verify text "Invalid email or password" is visible

title: Sign In button shows a loading state after submit
description: Verifies the sign-in button indicates progress after the user submits credentials.

title: Submit sign-in form using Enter key from the password field
description: Verifies keyboard submission triggers sign-in without clicking the button.

steps.description: Verify element "Create New Project" is visible

title: Missing email prevents sign-in and does not reach Dashboard
description: Verifies the sign-in form blocks submission (or shows validation) when email is missing.

title: Missing password prevents sign-in and does not reach Dashboard
description: Verifies the sign-in form blocks submission (or shows validation) when password is missing.

title: Sign in with an email that does not exist shows invalid credentials error
description: Verifies unknown user credentials are rejected with the same invalid-credentials message.

steps.description: Verify text "Invalid email or password" is visible

title: Navigate from Landing page to Sign In via the Sign in link
description: Verifies users can reach the sign-in page through in-app navigation from the landing page.

title: Sign out successfully from dashboard and land on sign-in page
description: Verifies a signed-in user can sign out via the navbar dropdown and is redirected to the sign-in page.

steps.description: Click avatar/user dropdown in the top-right navbar
Click "Sign out" button in dropdown menu

title: After sign out, direct navigation to dashboard sends user back to sign-in
description: Verifies the session is cleared after logout by checking dashboard access redirects to sign-in.

steps.description: Click avatar/user dropdown in the top-right navbar
Click "Sign out" button in dropdown menu

title: Sign out option is visible and accessible from the navbar dropdown
description: Verifies the user dropdown exposes a visible Sign out action while authenticated.

steps.description: Click avatar/user dropdown in the top-right navbar

title: Sign out from dashboard works even after interacting with the page content
description: Verifies logout remains reliable after typical dashboard usage (e.g., scrolling) and still redirects to sign-in.

steps.description: Scroll to the bottom of the page
Click avatar/user dropdown in the top-right navbar
Click "Sign out" button in dropdown menu

title: Open the sign-in page after logout and confirm sign-in UI is present
description: Verifies the post-logout destination is the credential sign-in page with the expected form visible.

steps.description: Click avatar/user dropdown in the top-right navbar
Click "Sign out" button in dropdown menu

title: Landing page renders key marketing sections
description: Verifies the landing page shows the hero content, workflow explanation, code preview, and features grid content.

steps.description: Verify text "Start Building - Free" is visible
Scroll to the three-step workflow section
Scroll to the code output preview section
Verify element "code output preview" is visible
Scroll to the features grid section

title: Primary hero CTA navigates to Sign up
description: Verifies clicking the main hero CTA sends the user to the sign-up page.

steps.description: Verify text "Start Building - Free" is visible

title: Top navigation Sign in link navigates to Sign in page
description: Verifies the navbar "Sign in" link routes to the email/password sign-in screen.

title: Top navigation Get started link navigates to Sign up page
description: Verifies the navbar "Get started" link routes to the sign-up screen.

title: Bottom CTA Get started free navigates to Sign up
description: Verifies the bottom-of-page CTA navigates to the sign-up page.

steps.description: Scroll to the bottom CTA section
Verify text "Get started free" is visible

title: View Documentation link is present and clickable
description: Verifies the landing page includes a "View Documentation" link and it is clickable.

title: Authenticated user is redirected away from Sign in to Dashboard
description: Verifies a signed-in user cannot stay on the sign-in page and is redirected to the dashboard.

title: Start from landing page nav to Sign in, then sign in successfully to reach Dashboard
description: Verifies the end-to-end path: landing page -> sign in page -> successful credential sign-in -> dashboard.

title: Navbar logo returns to Dashboard from an authenticated page
description: Verifies the MCPForge logo in the navbar navigates back to /dashboard while logged in.

steps.description: Click the MCPForge logo in the navbar

title: Dashboard link in navbar navigates to /dashboard
description: Verifies the "Dashboard" navbar link takes the user to the Dashboard page while logged in.

steps.description: Click the "Dashboard" link in the navbar

title: User menu dropdown displays signed-in user name and email
description: Verifies the avatar/name dropdown opens and shows the user's name and email.

steps.description: Click the avatar/name in the navbar to open the dropdown menu
Verify element "user dropdown menu" is visible

title: Sign out from user menu logs the user out
description: Verifies the Sign out action in the user dropdown ends the session and returns to the sign-in screen.

steps.description: Click the avatar/name in the navbar to open the dropdown menu
Verify element "user dropdown menu" is visible

title: User menu can be opened and closed without navigating away
description: Verifies the dropdown toggles open/closed and does not change the current page URL.

steps.description: Click the avatar/name in the navbar to open the dropdown menu
Verify element "user dropdown menu" is visible
Click the avatar/name in the navbar again to close the dropdown menu
Verify element "user dropdown menu" is not visible

title: Navbar remains visible after scrolling Dashboard
description: Verifies the navbar stays visible and usable after scrolling the authenticated page.

steps.description: Scroll to the bottom of the Dashboard page
Click the avatar/name in the navbar to open the dropdown menu
Verify element "user dropdown menu" is visible

title: Sign out action is accessible and visible in the user dropdown
description: Verifies the dropdown contains a visible "Sign out" option when opened.

steps.description: Click the avatar/name in the navbar to open the dropdown menu
Verify element "user dropdown menu" is visible

title: Dashboard displays project list after loading state
description: Verifies the dashboard shows a loading skeleton first and then renders a grid of project cards with expected summary fields.

steps.description: Click on "Dashboard" in the app navigation (or equivalent link to the dashboard)
Verify element "project cards grid" is visible

title: Project cards show key metadata fields
description: Verifies each project card shows project name, spec title, endpoint count, and last updated time in a visible format.

steps.description: Click on "Dashboard" in the app navigation (or equivalent link to the dashboard)
Verify element "a project card" is visible

title: Open a project by clicking a project card
description: Verifies clicking a project card navigates to the project detail page.

steps.description: Click on "Dashboard" in the app navigation (or equivalent link to the dashboard)
Verify element "a project card" is visible
Click on the first visible project card

title: Navigate to Create New Project from dashboard using New Project button
description: Verifies the dashboard New Project action takes the user to the create project page.

steps.description: Click on "Dashboard" in the app navigation (or equivalent link to the dashboard)

title: Dashboard remains usable after rapid reload: loading skeleton then content appears
description: Verifies a second visit to dashboard still shows a loading state then renders content (cards or empty state) without getting stuck.

steps.description: Click on "Dashboard" in the app navigation (or equivalent link to the dashboard)
Verify element "project cards grid" is visible

title: Create project successfully by pasting valid OpenAPI JSON
description: Verifies a user can create a new project via the Paste Content tab using valid OpenAPI JSON and is redirected to the new project page with a success toast.

steps.description: Type "My Paste JSON Project" into the "Project Name" field

title: Show JSON parsing error and stay on page when invalid JSON is pasted
description: Verifies invalid JSON triggers the visible parsing error alert and the user remains on the create project page.

steps.description: Type "Invalid JSON Project" into the "Project Name" field
Type the following into the spec textarea: "{ \"openapi\": \"3.0.0\", \"info\": { \"title\": \"Broken\", \"version\": \"1.0.0\" },"
Verify text "Invalid JSON syntax" is visible

title: Create project successfully by pasting valid OpenAPI YAML
description: Verifies a user can create a new project via the Paste Content tab using valid OpenAPI YAML content.

steps.description: Type "My Paste YAML Project" into the "Project Name" field

Type the following into the spec textarea: "openapi: 3.0.0
info:
  title: Pet API
  version: 1.0.0
paths:
  /pets:
    get:
      responses:
        '200':
          description: OK
"

title: Prevent creation when Project Name is missing (client-side validation)
description: Verifies the user cannot create a project from the Paste Content tab without providing a project name and sees a visible validation message.

title: Prevent creation when pasted content is empty
description: Verifies the user cannot create a project from the Paste Content tab when the textarea is empty and sees a visible error/validation message.

steps.description: Type "Empty Spec Project" into the "Project Name" field

title: Character count updates after pasting content and remains visible after edits
description: Verifies the character count is visible on the Paste Content tab and updates when the content is changed before creating the project.

steps.description: Type "Char Count Project" into the "Project Name" field

Type the following into the spec textarea: "openapi: 3.0.0
info:
  title: Pet API
  version: 1.0.0
paths: {}
"
Type "#" at the end of the spec textarea

title: Invalid YAML shows a visible parsing/validation error and no redirect occurs
description: Verifies invalid YAML content produces a visible error and the user stays on the create project page.

steps.description: Type "Invalid YAML Project" into the "Project Name" field

Type the following into the spec textarea: "openapi: 3.0.0
info
  title: Missing colon
paths:
  /pets:
    get: [this is not valid]
"

title: Fetch valid OpenAPI spec from URL and create project successfully
description: Verifies a user can fetch a valid OpenAPI spec from a URL, preview it, and create a new project that redirects to the project detail page.
category: Create Project - Fetch from URL Tab

steps.description: Click the "Create New Project" action on the dashboard
Enter a project name in the "Project Name" field
Enter the URL of an OpenAPI spec
Verify element "Fetch button loading spinner" is visible
Verify text "Spec fetched successfully" is visible
Verify element "Success toast notification" is visible

title: Invalid URL format shows validation error and does not fetch
description: Verifies the Fetch from URL tab validates URL format and shows an "Invalid URL format" error without fetching.
category: Create Project - Fetch from URL Tab

steps.description: Click the "Create New Project" action on the dashboard
Enter a project name in the "Project Name" field
Type "not-a-url" into the URL input field
Verify text "Invalid URL format" is visible
Verify text "Spec fetched successfully" is not visible

title: Cannot create project until spec is successfully fetched
description: Verifies the UI prevents project creation when no spec has been fetched (or indicates a visible error) and does not redirect away from /projects/new.
category: Create Project - Fetch from URL Tab

steps.description: Click the "Create New Project" action on the dashboard
Enter a project name in the "Project Name" field
Verify element "Spec preview" is not visible

title: Fetch fails due to unreachable URL shows a visible fetch error
description: Verifies a valid-looking URL that cannot be reached results in a visible error and no successful preview is shown.
category: Create Project - Fetch from URL Tab

steps.description: Click the "Create New Project" action on the dashboard
Enter a project name in the "Project Name" field
Type "https://example.invalid/openapi.yaml" into the URL input field
Verify element "Fetch error message" is visible
Verify text "Spec fetched successfully" is not visible

title: Changing URL and re-fetching updates the preview and retains success state
description: Verifies the user can fetch once successfully, then change the URL and fetch again, and the preview remains visible after the second success.
category: Create Project - Fetch from URL Tab

steps.description: Click the "Create New Project" action on the dashboard
Enter a project name in the "Project Name" field
Enter the URL of an OpenAPI spec
Verify text "Spec fetched successfully" is visible
Enter a different valid OpenAPI spec URL in the URL input field
Verify text "Spec fetched successfully" is visible

title: Change a single endpoint MCP type to Tool and observe successful save indicators

Click the MCP type dropdown on the first endpoint row
Select "Tool" from the MCP type dropdown
Verify text "Tool" is visible in the MCP type cell for that endpoint row
Verify element "Saving" (spinner icon) is visible in that endpoint row
Verify element "Saved" (green checkmark indicator) is visible in that endpoint row

Select "Resource" from the MCP type dropdown
Verify text "Resource" is visible in the MCP type cell for that endpoint row
Verify text "Resource" is visible within the summary counts section

Click the MCP type dropdown on the same endpoint row
Select "Resource" from the MCP type dropdown

Verify element "Saved" (green checkmark indicator) is visible in that endpoint row

title: Save shows spinner then success toast and updated name remains after refresh navigation
description: Verifies saving shows visible progress feedback and the updated name persists when returning to the dashboard and re-opening the project.

steps.description: Click on the first project card in the dashboard list
Click the pencil icon next to the project name
Type "Persistent Name" into the project name input field
Click the check icon to save
Click on the "Dashboard" navigation link (or app logo) to return to the dashboard
Click on the same project card again to reopen it

title: Attempt to save an empty project name shows failure feedback and reverts
description: Verifies saving an empty name results in visible error feedback and the displayed name reverts (no empty header name).

steps.description: Click on the first project card in the dashboard list
Click the pencil icon next to the project name
Type "" into the project name input field
Verify element "Project name text in header" is visible

title: Special characters in name save successfully and display correctly
description: Verifies a project name containing punctuation characters can be saved and is rendered in the header.

steps.description: Click on the first project card in the dashboard list
Click the pencil icon next to the project name
Type "MCPForge: Tools & Resources (v1.0)" into the project name input field
Verify text "MCPForge: Tools & Resources (v1.0)" is visible
